---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-worldmap3.png')
background-size: 105%
background-class: top

<br>

.size60[

**Today's Agenda**

- Final Paper Prep: Feedback on Outlines
]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2024)
]]

???

## Prep for class
1. Review submitted outlines on Canvas



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[.content-box-purple[**Final Exam (Due Dec 12, 10am)**]]

.size35[
Select a **NEW** international political event and make an argument that one of the IR theories / models we've studied in class this term "best" explains the event. 

Your paper must consider and evaluate how **THREE** of the IR theories from class explain your chosen event.

- No double-dipping on realism/liberalism

- At least one theory from the second half of class

- No events used in the prior paper
]

???

### Any questions on the final paper prompt?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.pull-left[

<br>
<br>

.size70[.center[**For Wednesday**]]

<br>
<br>

.size70[.center[**Draft an Outline**]]

]

.pull-right[

.size30[**Intro**
+ What is the question?
+ Why do we care?
+ Thesis statement
]

.size30[**Apply Theory 1**
+ Evidence
]

.size30[**Apply Theory 2**
+ Evidence
]

.size30[**Apply Theory 3**
+ Evidence
]]


???

### Does everybody have an outline?

*Split class into NEW small groups (no more than 3 each)*



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[.content-box-white[**Groups**]]

.size40[
15 minutes to discuss each outline

1. Is the group convinced by your pros/cons lists for each theory?

2. Any additional ideas for the lists?

3. Brainstorm real-world examples you could include to support your claims.
]

???

I'll keep the time for you and announce when to switch to each new paper.

<br>

### Questions on your job in class today?

Get to it!



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

.size80[
**Friday:**

**Writing Workshop**
]

???

Get some writing on paper and come to class ready to get and receive feedback!
